meaning: n.殴打,击败adj.巨大的,极好的adv.十足地
examples: They had to pay a walloping great fine.
Our team got a real walloping last week.
She threatened the children with a walloping.
These could make for a gift like none other or, the company says, lead to walloping financial returns of up to 300 percent.
After the invited walloping, we swam out.
